description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar energy installation featuring a large rectangular array of photovoltaic panels elevated above a patterned base. The solar panels are supported by a series of angled metal posts, providing stability and optimal tilt for sunlight capture. The base appears to be a textured platform, possibly simulating a rooftop or ground surface, with various geometric features and a sloped section labeled for drainage. The structure is primarily composed of metallic materials, likely aluminum or steel, with the solar panels themselves having a glassy, dark surface. There are no visible electrical inverters or cabling, and the installation is designed to be a fixed, non-movable infrastructure element. The overall design suggests it is intended for urban or suburban deployment, such as in parking lots, rooftops, or open community spaces.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array on a patterned platform, supported by angled metal posts and designed for urban energy generation. The structure features a large, dark photovoltaic surface and a sloped base for drainage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rectangular solar panel array elevated above a patterned base with a visible sloped section and support posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled metal posts supporting the solar panels and the extended patterned platform beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel array, the supporting structure, and the slope of the base labeled for drainage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full length of the solar array, support posts, and the patterned platform with a sloped edge.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30-35 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum posts
attribute_array_configuration: single row of large rectangular panels arranged side by side
